title: NARC Field Day
text:
This is a website that describes planning for the ARRL Field Day operation conducted by the Nittany Amateur Radio Club (NARC). Rick Gilmore W3TM created and maintains the site.
Please see separate pages that describe what Field Day is about, the people who are involved, what they do when to make Field Day happen, and some of the material items needed to pull-off an event like this.



Antennas at sunset.








Note



Field Day 2025 is on Saturday, June 28, 2025 to Sunday, June 29, 2025.
The event runs for 24 hours beginning at 2:00 PM EDT (1800 UTC) on Saturday to 2:00 PM EDT (1800 UTC) on Sunday.

title: About
text:
Field Day is an event organized by the American Radio Relay League (ARRL), the membership organization for amateur radio operators (hams) in the U.S.
Field Day is an open house to show-off amateur radio to the public, an exercise in portable or field radio operations, and a contest to see which stations can earn the most points.
NARC will operate under the club callsign W3YA with two high frequency (HF) transmitters capable of long-distance communication.
We may have a bonus VHF/UHF station depending on member interest.
We will operate from our clubhouse location on top of Pine Grove Mountain off of State Route 26, making this a class 2D (two delta) operation.



NARC Clubhouse

title: Results
text:
About
This page provides some data about the club’s activities and results on Field Day.

title: Results
Results
text:
QSOs/transmitter


Code
w3ya |>
  dplyr::mutate(qs_per_xmtr = tot_qsos/n_xmtrs) |>
  ggplot() +
  aes(x = year, y = qs_per_xmtr) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2012, 2025, by = 1))










Participants


Code
w3ya |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(n_particip)) |>
  ggplot() +
  aes(x = year, y = n_particip) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2012, 2025, by = 1))


`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'


Code
  ylim(0,60)


<ScaleContinuousPosition>
 Range:  
 Limits:    0 --   60








Figure 3.7: Nittany ARC (W3YA) Field Day participants by year

title: Results
text:
QSOs/participant


Code
w3ya |>
  dplyr::mutate(qs_per_person = tot_qsos/n_particip) |>
  ggplot() +
  aes(x = year, y = qs_per_person) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2012, 2025, by = 1))

title: Recent Years
text:
About
This page summarizes data about club member participation in 2024-25.

title: Recent Years
text:
Setup

library(readr)

title: Recent Years
text:
Gather data

fd_24 <- readr::read_csv(
  "csv/narc-fd-2024-participation.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  col_types = cols(
    callsign = "c",
    attended_sat = "l",
    attended_sun = "l",
    operated_fd = "l",
    helped_out = "l"
  )
)

Warning: One or more parsing issues, call `problems()` on your data frame for details,
e.g.:
  dat <- vroom(...)
  problems(dat)

fd_24 <- fd_24 |>
  dplyr::mutate(attended = attended_sat | attended_sun)


fd_25 <- readr::read_csv(
  "csv/narc-field-day-2025-participation.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  col_types = cols(
    callsign = "c",
    member = "l",
    operated_fd = "l",
    setup_cleanup = "l",
    late_overnight = "l",
    pr = "l",
    food_drink = "l",
    education = "l",
    attended_sat = "l",
    attended_sun = "l",
    operated_fd = "l"
  )
)

fd_25 <- fd_25 |>
  dplyr::mutate(helped_out = setup_cleanup | pr | food_drink | education )


fd_24 <- fd_24 |>
  dplyr::select(callsign, attended_sat, attended_sun, operated_fd, helped_out) |>
  dplyr::mutate(year = 2024,
                callsign = toupper(callsign))

fd_25 <- fd_25 |>
  dplyr::select(callsign, attended_sat, attended_sun, operated_fd, helped_out) |>
  dplyr::mutate(year = 2025,
                callsign = toupper(callsign))

fd_24_25 <- rbind(fd_24, fd_25) |>
  dplyr::mutate(attended = attended_sat | attended_sun)

title: Recent Years
text:
Summarize

xtabs(~ attended + year, fd_24_25)

        year
attended 2024 2025
   FALSE   12    0
   TRUE    24   41

xtabs(~ operated_fd + year, fd_24_25)

           year
operated_fd 2024 2025
      FALSE   23   29
      TRUE    13   12

xtabs(~ helped_out + year, fd_24_25)

          year
helped_out 2024 2025
     FALSE   24    0
     TRUE    12   14

title: Stations
text:
About
This page describes some details about our Field Day stations.

title: Stations
text:
Class
Based on the results of the survey, we plan to operate three (2) transmitters from the clubhouse. Because we are operating from a fixed location on mains power, we are class D.
We will operate at 100 W or 500 W and use W3YA as our callsign.

title: Stations
text:
NARC Mountaintop
Both NARC1 and NARC2 will be ready for use on Field Day.

NARC1

ICOM IC-7610 transceiver
HP All-in-one PC with 2nd Display
KPA-1500 amplifier/ATU
ICOM SM-50 mic



NARC2

FlexRadio Flex 6400M
HP All-in-one PC with 2nd Display
KPA-1500 amplifier/ATU
Halibut Electronics audio interface
Inrad mic



Antennas

2x Mosley Triband Yagis, one pointed south, the other pointed west. These antennas will be for 20m, 15m, and 10m.
1 80m-10m EFHW antenna. This antenna is primarily for 40m and 75m/80m operation.
